summary: Root-cause fix for hamsa's mt7925e WiFi kernel panics delivered: bumped the nixpkgs flake input to kernel 7.1.3 (contains merged upstream fix 20b126920a25 for the sta_poll_list corruption race) and added kernel.panic_on_oops=1 to modules/system/boot.nix to close the pstore diagnostics gap. Phase 2 abort gate passed (kernel resolved to 7.1.3 on the first nix flake update nixpkgs), so the conditional boot.kernelPatches fallback (Phase 6) was not needed. nix flake check and nixos-rebuild build --flake .#hamsa both succeeded; the kernel was confirmed sourced from the Hydra binary cache (nix log shows no local build log for the linux-7.1.3 derivation). Activation (sudo nixos-rebuild switch + reboot) is deliberately NOT performed by this implementer, per the plan's explicit Non-Goal and risk mitigation requiring user consent for system activation.

blockers.description: System activation requires sudo and is out of scope for autonomous execution by design (plan Non-Goal + risk mitigation 'Activation run without user consent'). The user must run: sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#hamsa, then reboot, then verify uname -r >= 7.1.3 and the panic sysctls.
blockers.note: Not a true blocker on task completion from the implementer's perspective -- the plan's Definition of Done only requires the build be verified and activation be flagged, both of which are done. Flagged here for orchestrator/user visibility.

phases.phase_5_activation_user_confirmed: PARTIAL (flagged for user; post-reboot verification deferred)

next_action_hint: Task 106's repo-side implementation is complete and build-verified. Recommend transitioning task status toward completion (not blocked/partial in the state.json sense) since the plan's Definition of Done treats user activation as an explicit out-of-scope follow-up, not a phase the implementer must finish. If task status tracking requires activation before COMPLETED, keep status as implementing/partial and note the single remaining user step: sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#hamsa && reboot, then verify per the summary's User Action Required section.

artifacts.summary: nixpkgs input bumped to kernel 7.1.3 (nixpkgs-unstable pin untouched)
Updated with per-phase completion evidence and deviation annotations
Full implementation summary with verification, user action required, and monitoring/escalation guidance
